Eligible Population: Medicare Part B beneficiaries; >12 months since Part B effective date; no IPPE or AWV in past 12 months
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag Medicare patients without G0438/G0439 claim in past 12 months; check eligibility window from Part B enrollment date
Documentation Requirements: Health Risk Assessment (HRA), review of functional ability and safety, detection of cognitive impairment, personalized prevention plan, screening schedule list, advance directive discussion
Notes / Gotchas: First AWV only — subsequent visits use G0439. Can bill G2211 add-on if longitudinal relationship criteria met.

Eligible Population: Medicare Part B beneficiaries who have had a previous AWV or IPPE
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag Medicare patients with prior AWV but no AWV in past 12 months
Documentation Requirements: Updated HRA, updated prevention plan, updated screening schedule, cognitive assessment, advance care planning discussion
Notes / Gotchas: Most common AWV code in established practices. Pair with screening add-ons below.

Billing Opportunity: Initial Preventive Physical Exam (IPPE / Welcome to Medicare)
Eligible Population: New Medicare Part B beneficiaries within first 12 months of Part B coverage
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag new Medicare patients with Part B effective date within last 12 months and no prior IPPE
Documentation Requirements: Review of medical/surgical history, review of potential risk factors for depression, review of functional ability/safety, EKG, education/counseling, referral list
Notes / Gotchas: Must occur within first 12 months of Part B enrollment. Cannot bill both IPPE and AWV in same 12-month period.

Billing Opportunity: Advance Care Planning (as part of AWV)
Eligible Population: All Medicare beneficiaries (especially during AWV)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag all AWV visits; prompt provider to document ACP discussion; flag patients >65 without advance directive on file
Documentation Requirements: Document voluntary discussion of advance directives, healthcare proxy, living will, code status; 99497 = first 30 min, 99498 = each additional 30 min
Notes / Gotchas: Zero cost-share ONLY when billed as part of AWV. If billed standalone, normal cost-sharing applies. Time-based — document start/stop times.

Billing Opportunity: Personalized Prevention Plan of Service (PPPS) Update
Eligible Population: Medicare beneficiaries receiving AWV or IPPE
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Auto-include when AWV/IPPE is billed
Documentation Requirements: Update to personalized prevention plan as part of AWV/IPPE
Notes / Gotchas: Bundled into AWV payment — not separately reimbursed but required for compliant billing.

Eligible Population: All women, annually from adolescence through lifespan
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag female patients without preventive visit in past 12 months; age-appropriate code selection
Documentation Requirements: Age-appropriate screening services, contraception counseling, breast exam, pelvic exam as indicated, preventive counseling
Notes / Gotchas: Women's Preventive Services Initiative recommendation. Use modifier 33 for zero cost-sharing.

Eligible Population: All patients, age-stratified code selection
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients without preventive visit in past 12 months; select code by age bracket
Documentation Requirements: Comprehensive history, examination, anticipatory guidance, risk factor reduction, age/gender appropriate labs and screening orders

Eligible Population: Medicare patients seen for E/M visit (99202-99215) when the visit relates to a condition that is being managed in an ongoing longitudinal relationship
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag all Medicare E/M visits where patient has ≥1 chronic condition managed by the practice; auto-suggest when qualifying conditions exist in problem list
Documentation Requirements: Relationship must be ongoing (not first visit for new problem); condition requires ongoing management decision-making; document the medical decision-making complexity and relationship continuity
Notes / Gotchas: Cannot be billed with modifier 25 E/M. Cannot be billed with preventive visits. Pays ~$16.05 per visit — small per event but high volume. CMS began paying Jan 2024.

Eligible Population: Patients requiring E/M visit time exceeding the highest-level code threshold (99205: >74 min or 99215: >54 min total time)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag visits where encounter timer exceeds 54 min (established) or 74 min (new); prompt for 99417 documentation
Documentation Requirements: Must bill highest-level E/M first (99205 or 99215); document total time on date of encounter; 99417 for each additional 15 min (or part thereof)
Notes / Gotchas: Each unit = 15 minutes beyond threshold. Can bill multiple units. Medicare uses 99417; some private payers use 99354-99355 (check payer).

Eligible Population: Medicare beneficiaries whose preventive visit extends beyond the typical service time
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag preventive visits where encounter timer exceeds typical time for the billed preventive code
Documentation Requirements: G0513 = first 30 min beyond typical; G0514 = each additional 30 min; document total time
Notes / Gotchas: Use with AWV or IPPE when additional time is spent. Time-based — document start/stop.

Eligible Population: Medicare patients with 2+ chronic conditions expected to last ≥12 months that place the patient at significant risk of death, acute exacerbation, or functional decline
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag Medicare patients with ≥2 qualifying chronic ICD-10 codes on active problem list; check no CCM claim in current calendar month; verify patient consent on file
Documentation Requirements: Written patient consent, comprehensive care plan, 20+ min of clinical staff time per calendar month (non-face-to-face), care coordination documentation
Notes / Gotchas: Only one provider can bill CCM per patient per month. Patient must consent. MA/RN time counts. Big revenue multiplier over a panel.

Eligible Population: Same as 99490 but requiring ≥60 min of clinical staff time per month, with physician/NP personally performing substantial portion
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag CCM-eligible patients where monthly care coordination time logs approach/exceed 60 min; prompt upgrade from 99490
Documentation Requirements: Same as 99490 plus: provider personally performs substantial care management activities, 60+ min total clinical staff time, more complex care plan
Notes / Gotchas: Higher reimbursement than 99490. Requires provider personal involvement — not just delegated staff time.

Eligible Population: Medicare patients already qualifying for 99490, with additional 30 min of clinical staff time in same month
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag when CCM time logs for a 99490 patient reach 40+ min in a month; can be billed up to 2 additional units
Documentation Requirements: Document additional 30 min increments of clinical staff time beyond the initial 20 min
Notes / Gotchas: Add-on to 99490. Up to 2 units per month (i.e., 20 + 30 + 30 = 80 min max with 99490 + 2x99439).

Eligible Population: Medicare patients with 1 complex chronic condition requiring ongoing management
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag Medicare patients with a single dominant chronic condition (e.g., CHF, COPD, CKD) where CCM 2-condition threshold is not met
Documentation Requirements: 99424 = first 30 min, 99425 = each additional 30 min; single complex condition care plan; clinical staff time per calendar month
Notes / Gotchas: Alternative to CCM for patients with only 1 qualifying condition. Cannot bill both PCM and CCM for same patient in same month.

Eligible Population: Patients discharged from hospital, SNF, observation, or partial hospitalization within past 30 days with high medical decision-making complexity
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Monitor hospital/SNF discharge notifications; flag patients with recent inpatient stay; verify face-to-face visit within 7 days and phone/electronic contact within 2 business days of discharge
Documentation Requirements: Contact within 2 business days of discharge (phone/electronic), face-to-face visit within 7 calendar days, medication reconciliation, care coordination for 30-day period, high-complexity MDM
Notes / Gotchas: Extremely high-value code. Requires proactive discharge tracking. The 2-business-day contact is the most commonly missed requirement.

Eligible Population: Same as 99496 but moderate medical decision-making complexity
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Same discharge tracking as 99496; flag when MDM is moderate rather than high
Documentation Requirements: Same requirements as 99496 but face-to-face visit within 14 calendar days (instead of 7) and moderate-complexity MDM
Notes / Gotchas: Lower bar than 99496. If you miss the 7-day window for 99496, you can still capture 99495 within 14 days.

Eligible Population: Medicare patients with behavioral health condition (depression, anxiety, SUD, etc.) requiring 20+ min of clinical staff time per month for BH care management
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag Medicare patients with active behavioral health ICD-10 codes (F20-F48, F10-F19, etc.) on problem list; track monthly BH care coordination time
Documentation Requirements: Initial assessment, care plan, 20+ min/month of BH care manager time, systematic monitoring with validated tools (PHQ-9, GAD-7), treatment adjustment
Notes / Gotchas: Cannot bill same month as CoCM (99492-99494). Lower documentation bar than CoCM.

Eligible Population: Medicare patients with BH condition enrolled in collaborative care model with psychiatric consultant
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag BH-eligible patients where practice has collaborative care infrastructure; track initial 36+ min per month
Documentation Requirements: Psychiatric consultant oversight, behavioral health care manager, 36+ min first calendar month, systematic care tracking, patient registry, measurement-based care with validated tools
Notes / Gotchas: Requires practice to have CoCM infrastructure: BH care manager + psychiatric consultant. Higher reimbursement than 99484.

Eligible Population: Same as 99492, subsequent months
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag CoCM patients in months 2+; track 36+ min per month
Documentation Requirements: Same as 99492 for subsequent months; 36+ min clinical staff time
Notes / Gotchas: Ongoing monthly billing for patients actively in CoCM.

Eligible Population: CoCM patients with additional 30 min of clinical staff time in a month
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag when CoCM time logs exceed 36 min threshold; bill as add-on
Documentation Requirements: Additional 30 min of BH care manager time beyond initial 36 min
Notes / Gotchas: Add-on to 99492 or 99493.

Eligible Population: All adults (USPSTF A recommendation); Medicare: all beneficiaries; Private: general adult population including pregnant/postpartum
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag all adult patients at each visit or annually; auto-prompt for PHQ-2/PHQ-9 administration; flag result for documentation
Documentation Requirements: Standardized screening instrument, score documented in chart, brief interpretation, follow-up plan if positive
Notes / Gotchas: Can bill up to 2 units per encounter for different screening domains (e.g., PHQ-9 + GAD-7). Medicare also has G0444 for annual depression screening.

Eligible Population: Adults with risk factors or as part of routine behavioral health screening
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients with anxiety risk factors or as second screening instrument alongside PHQ-9
Documentation Requirements: Same as depression screening; use validated GAD-7 instrument
Notes / Gotchas: Second unit of 96127 when administered same visit as PHQ-9.

Eligible Population: Children: 9 mo, 18 mo (developmental + autism), 24 mo (autism), 30 mo (developmental)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag pediatric patients at Bright Futures milestone ages; prompt for ASQ-3 or M-CHAT-R administration
Documentation Requirements: Standardized developmental screening instrument (ASQ-3, PEDS, M-CHAT-R/F), score documented, interpretation, referral if indicated
Notes / Gotchas: Bright Futures schedule. 96110 for developmental; 96127 can also be used for emotional/behavioral. Do not use 96110 for adolescent behavioral screens.

Eligible Population: Children and adolescents at each preventive visit (Bright Futures)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag pediatric/adolescent patients at preventive visits; age-appropriate tool selection (PSC for younger, CRAFFT for adolescents)
Documentation Requirements: Pediatric Symptom Checklist (PSC), CRAFFT (substance use), or other validated tool; score, interpretation, follow-up
Notes / Gotchas: Bright Futures recommends behavioral assessment at every well visit newborn through 21 years.

Eligible Population: Medicare: all beneficiaries annually; Private: all adults 18+
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag adult patients without alcohol screening in past 12 months; prompt AUDIT-C or CAGE administration
Documentation Requirements: Validated screening tool (AUDIT-C, CAGE, AUDIT), score documented; G0442 = screening, G0443 = counseling if positive; Private: 99408 (15-30 min), 99409 (>30 min)
Notes / Gotchas: Medicare: G0442 (screening) + G0443 (counseling if positive, up to 4 sessions/year). Private: 99408/99409 with modifier 33.

Eligible Population: Adults 18+ (USPSTF B recommendation)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag adult patients without substance use screening; prompt DAST-10 or NIDA Quick Screen
Documentation Requirements: Validated screening tool, score, interpretation, brief intervention if positive; 99408 = 15-30 min, 99409 = >30 min
Notes / Gotchas: SBIRT (Screening, Brief Intervention, Referral to Treatment) model. Can be lucrative if practice documents the brief intervention time.

Eligible Population: All adults; pregnant women (expanded counseling)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients with active tobacco use on social history or problem list; prompt for cessation counseling documentation
Documentation Requirements: 99406 = 3-10 min counseling, 99407 = >10 min counseling; document time, intervention, patient response, plan
Notes / Gotchas: Medicare: up to 8 sessions per 12-month period (2 quit attempts, 4 sessions each). High-value for smoker-heavy panels.

Eligible Population: AWV patients (cognitive impairment detection required); patients with cognitive complaints
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag all AWV patients for cognitive screening; flag patients with memory complaints or dementia risk factors
Documentation Requirements: 96127 for brief screening (MMSE, MoCA, Mini-Cog); 99483 for comprehensive cognitive assessment (requires full evaluation with care plan)
Notes / Gotchas: 96127 for brief screening during AWV. 99483 (~$280) for comprehensive evaluation — much higher reimbursement but requires extensive documentation and ~50 min.

Eligible Population: Postpartum women at well-baby visits (Bright Futures); pregnant/postpartum women (USPSTF)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag mothers of newborns/infants at well-baby visits; flag pregnant patients; prompt Edinburgh or PHQ-9
Documentation Requirements: Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale (EPDS) or PHQ-9; score documented, interpretation, referral if positive
Notes / Gotchas: Billable at well-baby visits under the mother's chart or baby's chart depending on payer policy. Bright Futures + USPSTF + WPSI all recommend.

Eligible Population: Medicare: all beneficiaries without CVD symptoms; Private: adults with risk factors per USPSTF
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients without lipid panel in appropriate interval; age/risk-based recommendation engine
Documentation Requirements: Lipid panel: total cholesterol, LDL, HDL, triglycerides; order with appropriate diagnosis code (Z13.220)
Notes / Gotchas: Medicare covers every 5 years (or more frequently with risk factors). Private covers per USPSTF guidelines with modifier 33.

Eligible Population: Medicare: beneficiaries with risk factors for diabetes or pre-diabetes; Private: adults 40-70 who are overweight/obese (USPSTF)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients aged 40-70 with BMI ≥25 without glucose or A1C in past 3 years; flag patients with pre-diabetes risk factors
Frequency: Every 3 years (or annually with risk factors)
Documentation Requirements: Fasting glucose (82947), glucose tolerance test (82951), or HbA1c (83036); document indication and risk factors
Notes / Gotchas: Private payers: USPSTF recommends as part of cardiovascular risk assessment. Medicare has separate diabetes screening benefit.

Eligible Population: Medicare: adults at high risk, born 1945-1965, or had blood transfusion before 1992; Private: adults 18-79 (USPSTF)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag adults 18-79 without HCV screening on record; prioritize birth cohort 1945-1965
Documentation Requirements: HCV antibody test; if positive, confirmatory RNA test; document risk factors or universal screening indication
Notes / Gotchas: USPSTF expanded to universal screening for all adults 18-79 in 2020. Previously just the 1945-1965 birth cohort.

Eligible Population: Medicare: pregnant women (first prenatal), high-risk non-pregnant; Private: persons at high risk (USPSTF)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag high-risk patients (IVDU history, endemic country origin, HIV+, household contacts) and pregnant patients without HBV screening
Documentation Requirements: HBsAg, anti-HBs, anti-HBc; document risk factors
Notes / Gotchas: Screening includes triple panel: surface antigen, surface antibody, core antibody. Vaccine follow-up if susceptible.

Eligible Population: Medicare: all beneficiaries regardless of risk, including pregnant women; Private: adolescents/adults 15-65 (USPSTF)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients 15-65 without HIV screening on record; flag all pregnant patients; flag high-risk patients for repeat screening
Documentation Requirements: HIV antigen/antibody combination test preferred; document screening indication
Notes / Gotchas: USPSTF A recommendation for ages 15-65 and all pregnant women. Medicare covers annually for high-risk, once for others.

Eligible Population: Women ≤24 or at higher risk (chlamydia/gonorrhea); all persons at increased risk (syphilis); pregnant women (all three)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag sexually active women ≤24; flag patients with STI risk factors; flag all pregnant patients for syphilis/chlamydia/gonorrhea
Documentation Requirements: Chlamydia: 87490-87491; Gonorrhea: 87590-87591; Syphilis: 86592-86593, 86780; document risk factors or pregnancy status
Notes / Gotchas: Multiple codes per encounter possible. Syphilis screening is USPSTF A recommendation for high-risk + all pregnant women.

Eligible Population: Adults 45-75 (USPSTF); Medicare: aged 50-85 (some tests)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients 45-75 without CRC screening per recommended interval (FIT annual, Cologuard q3y, colonoscopy q10y)
Documentation Requirements: Order appropriate screening test; document average-risk or high-risk status; for Medicare: different HCPCS codes for different modalities
Notes / Gotchas: Cologuard (81528) is high-margin for reference labs. Office-based FIT (82270/82274) is low cost to administer. Colonoscopy referrals generate downstream revenue.

Eligible Population: Adults 50-80 (USPSTF) or 55-77 (Medicare) with ≥30 pack-year smoking history and currently smoking or quit within 15 years
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients with ≥30 pack-year smoking history in social history; age 50-80; currently smoking or quit <15 years; no LDCT in past 12 months
Documentation Requirements: Medicare: G0296 (counseling visit) + G0297 (LDCT); written order meeting CMS requirements; document pack-year history and quit date; shared decision-making
Notes / Gotchas: G0296 counseling visit is separately billable and often missed. The LDCT itself is ordered to radiology. The office captures the counseling code.

Eligible Population: Women 21-65 (Pap q3y ages 21-29; Pap+HPV co-test or HPV alone q5y ages 30-65)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag female patients 21-65 without cervical screening per interval; age-based method recommendation
Documentation Requirements: Pap smear collection (Q0091 for Medicare); cytology processing; HPV co-testing if age 30+; document screening interval compliance
Notes / Gotchas: Medicare: Q0091 is the specimen collection code. Can be billed with screening Pap codes. HPV co-test (G0476) for women 30-65.

Eligible Population: Women ≥40 (every 1-2 years per USPSTF); Medicare: women ≥35
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag female patients ≥40 without mammography in past 1-2 years; generate referral prompt
Documentation Requirements: Order screening mammography; document screening indication; ensure referral is placed
Notes / Gotchas: Office captures the order placement and may bill for the associated E/M if problems are discussed. Imaging center bills the mammography itself.

Eligible Population: Medicare: estrogen-deficient women at osteoporosis risk, vertebral abnormalities, glucocorticoid therapy, hyperparathyroidism; Private: postmenopausal women <65 at increased risk, all women ≥65
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag women ≥65 without DEXA on file; flag postmenopausal women <65 with risk factors (FRAX score); flag glucocorticoid patients
Frequency: Every 2 years (Medicare) or per risk assessment
Documentation Requirements: Order DEXA scan; document risk factors or indication; interpret results if performed in office
Notes / Gotchas: If office has DEXA machine, this is direct revenue. If referral, office captures the E/M and care gap closure.

Eligible Population: Men 65-75 who have ever smoked (USPSTF B recommendation)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag male patients 65-75 with smoking history and no prior AAA screening
Documentation Requirements: Order abdominal ultrasound for AAA screening; document smoking history; one-time screening benefit
Notes / Gotchas: Referral to ultrasound. Medicare covers as part of IPPE or with physician referral. One-time benefit — high-value to capture before the window closes.

Eligible Population: Adults at increased risk (USPSTF B recommendation): recent immigrants, homeless, incarcerated, healthcare workers, immunocompromised
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients with TB risk factors in social history without recent TB screening
Documentation Requirements: TST (86580) or IGRA (86480/86481); document risk factors for screening indication
Notes / Gotchas: QuantiFERON (IGRA) is increasingly preferred over TST. Document the specific risk factor triggering the screen.

Eligible Population: All patients ≥6 months annually; Medicare: all beneficiaries
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients without flu vaccine documented in current season (Sept-March); prompt at every visit during flu season
Documentation Requirements: Vaccine product code + administration code; document lot number, site, route, VIS date; Medicare uses G0008 for admin
Notes / Gotchas: Vaccine cost + administration fee. High-dose (90662) or adjuvanted (90653) for ≥65. Revenue from both vaccine purchase margin and admin fee.

Eligible Population: Adults ≥65; adults 19-64 with risk conditions; Medicare: all beneficiaries
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag adults ≥65 without PCV20 on record; flag younger adults with immunocompromising conditions, cochlear implants, CSF leaks, chronic conditions
Documentation Requirements: PCV20 preferred as single dose for most adults ≥65; document prior pneumococcal vaccination history; Medicare uses G0009 admin code
Notes / Gotchas: ACIP 2023 simplified recommendations: PCV20 alone for most adults ≥65. Check prior PCV13/PPSV23 history for dosing decisions.

Eligible Population: Adults ≥50 (2-dose series); immunocompromised adults ≥19
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag adults ≥50 without Shingrix series completion; track dose 1 vs dose 2 status
Documentation Requirements: Recombinant zoster vaccine; 2 doses, 2-6 months apart; document lot, site, route, VIS; Part D coverage (not Part B)
Notes / Gotchas: Part D benefit — patient may have copay depending on plan. Office still collects admin fee and often vaccine margin. Track series completion.

Eligible Population: All adults (Tdap once, then Td/Tdap q10y); pregnant women (Tdap each pregnancy at 27-36 weeks)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag adults without Tdap on record; flag adults with last tetanus >10 years ago; flag pregnant patients at 27-36 weeks
Documentation Requirements: Document vaccine product, lot, site, route, VIS date; for pregnant patients document gestational age
Notes / Gotchas: Frequently missed in adult patients. Pregnancy dosing is a separate billable event each pregnancy.

Eligible Population: Ages 9-26 (routine); 27-45 (shared clinical decision-making)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients 9-26 without HPV series completion; for 27-45, flag if patient requests or has risk factors
Documentation Requirements: Document dose number, lot, site, route, VIS; 2 doses if started before 15th birthday, 3 doses if started ≥15
Notes / Gotchas: High vaccine cost = high margin opportunity. Ages 27-45 require shared decision-making discussion documentation.

Eligible Population: Medicare: beneficiaries at intermediate/high risk for HBV; Private: all unvaccinated adults (ACIP universal recommendation 2022)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag adults without documented HBV vaccination or immunity; prioritize high-risk patients
Documentation Requirements: Heplisav-B (90739, 2-dose) or Engerix-B/Recombivax (90746/90740, 3-dose); document series tracking; Medicare uses G0010 admin
Notes / Gotchas: ACIP 2022 universal adult HBV vaccination recommendation expanded the eligible population significantly. Heplisav-B (2 doses) simplifies completion.

Eligible Population: All children 12-23 months; adults at risk or requesting vaccination
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag unvaccinated adults with travel to endemic areas, chronic liver disease, MSM, IVDU, homelessness
Documentation Requirements: Document lot, site, route, VIS; 2-dose series 6-12 months apart
Notes / Gotchas: ACIP recommends for all children and adults at increased risk. Twinrix (90636) combines Hep A + Hep B.

Eligible Population: Per current CDC/ACIP recommendations
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients without current COVID-19 vaccination per latest ACIP schedule
Documentation Requirements: Document per current ACIP guidelines; codes update frequently — check CMS transmittals
Notes / Gotchas: Codes and coverage change frequently. NP Companion should pull from CDC immunization schedule API for current recommendations.

Eligible Population: Adults ≥60 (shared clinical decision); pregnant women 32-36 weeks (seasonal)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag adults ≥60 without RSV vaccination; flag pregnant patients at 32-36 weeks during RSV season
Documentation Requirements: Document shared clinical decision-making for adults ≥60; for pregnant patients document gestational age and RSV season timing
Notes / Gotchas: Newer vaccine — verify current ACIP recommendations and payer coverage. Part D for Medicare.

Eligible Population: Adolescents 11-12 (MenACWY, booster 16); 16-23 (MenB shared decision); adults at increased risk
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag adolescents 11-12 without MenACWY; flag 16-year-olds for booster; flag college-bound patients
Documentation Requirements: MenACWY: 2 doses (11-12 + 16); MenB: 2-3 dose series for at-risk; document lot, site, route, VIS
Notes / Gotchas: Often missed at the 16-year-old booster dose. College entry requirements drive catch-up vaccination.

Eligible Population: Adults with BMI ≥30; Medicare: IBT for Obesity (G0447); Private: USPSTF recommendation
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag adults with BMI ≥30 on most recent vitals; prompt for behavioral counseling documentation; track counseling sessions
Documentation Requirements: Medicare IBT: G0447 (15 min face-to-face, up to 22 sessions in 12 months); Private: 97802-97804 with modifier 33; document BMI, counseling content, behavioral goals
Notes / Gotchas: Medicare IBT (G0447): 6 sessions monthly, then 6 every 2 weeks, then monthly for 6 months IF ≥3kg weight loss at 6 months. Very specific schedule.

Eligible Population: Children and adolescents ≥6 years with obesity (USPSTF)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag pediatric patients ≥6 with BMI ≥95th percentile; prompt for intensive behavioral intervention referral or in-office counseling
Documentation Requirements: USPSTF recommends ≥26 contact hours for intensive behavioral interventions; document BMI percentile, counseling provided, goals set
Notes / Gotchas: USPSTF recommends referral to comprehensive, intensive behavioral interventions. In-office counseling is billable.

Eligible Population: Medicare beneficiaries with diabetes, renal disease, or post-kidney transplant (within 36 months); requires physician referral to RD/nutrition professional
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag Medicare patients with diabetes (E11.x) or CKD (N18.x) without MNT referral; flag post-transplant patients
Frequency: 3 hours initial year, 2 hours subsequent years
Documentation Requirements: Physician referral required; services by registered dietitian or nutrition professional; 97802 (initial 15 min), 97803 (re-assessment 15 min), 97804 (group); Medicare: G0270/G0271
Notes / Gotchas: Office captures the referral and any associated E/M. If the practice employs an RD, direct MNT billing. Otherwise, referral revenue.

Eligible Population: Medicare beneficiaries with diabetes diagnosis + physician/NP order for DSMT
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag Medicare diabetic patients without DSMT in past 12 months; generate DSMT referral/order prompt
Documentation Requirements: Physician/NP order required; document diabetes diagnosis and DSMT order; G0108 (individual), G0109 (group, per patient)
Notes / Gotchas: Not zero cost-share, but generates revenue if practice has certified diabetes educator (CDE/CDCES) or DSMT program.

Eligible Population: All Medicare beneficiaries who are competent and alert; provided by PCP in primary care setting
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag Medicare patients with CVD risk factors (HTN, hyperlipidemia, obesity, diabetes, smoking); annual counseling prompt
Documentation Requirements: One face-to-face visit per year; encourage aspirin use (if appropriate), screen for HTN, intensive behavioral counseling for diet/exercise; document counseling time and content
Notes / Gotchas: Often overlooked. Can be billed alongside AWV. Document the specific CVD counseling separately from other visit components.

Eligible Population: All Medicare beneficiaries who use tobacco
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag Medicare patients with tobacco use in social history; prompt cessation counseling documentation
Documentation Requirements: 99406 (3-10 min), 99407 (>10 min); 2 quit attempts, 4 sessions each; document tobacco type, counseling time, intervention, plan
Notes / Gotchas: Zero cost-share for Medicare. Can be billed at every visit where counseling is provided. Combine with NRT prescriptions.

Eligible Population: All adolescent and adult women with reproductive capacity
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag female patients of reproductive age without documented contraception counseling in past year
Documentation Requirements: Women's Preventive Services Initiative recommendation; document contraceptive counseling, method discussion, patient preference; typically part of well-woman visit
Notes / Gotchas: Part of the well-woman visit bundle. Ensure documentation specifically addresses contraception to support coding.

Eligible Population: Pregnant and nursing women
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag pregnant and postpartum patients; prompt for breastfeeding counseling documentation
Frequency: As needed during pregnancy and postpartum
Documentation Requirements: USPSTF/WPSI recommendation; counseling by trained provider; document counseling content, patient questions, referrals to lactation consultants
Notes / Gotchas: Part of ACA preventive benefit. If practice has lactation consultant, direct billing opportunity.

Eligible Population: Fair-skinned persons aged 6 months to 24 years (USPSTF B recommendation)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag pediatric/young adult patients with fair skin at preventive visits
Documentation Requirements: Counsel on minimizing UV radiation exposure; document counseling provided
Notes / Gotchas: Can be documented as part of anticipatory guidance during well-child/well-adult visits. Supports preventive coding compliance.

Eligible Population: Community-dwelling adults ≥65 at increased risk for falls (USPSTF B recommendation)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients ≥65 with fall risk factors (balance issues, polypharmacy, prior falls, vitamin D deficiency)
Documentation Requirements: Exercise interventions to prevent falls; PT referral; vitamin D supplementation; document fall risk assessment and intervention plan
Notes / Gotchas: If practice provides PT services, direct billing. Otherwise, referral + documentation of fall risk assessment at the visit.

Eligible Population: Medicare patients with acute or chronic conditions requiring remote monitoring (e.g., HTN, CHF, DM, COPD)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients with HTN, CHF, DM, or COPD who could benefit from home BP monitors, glucose monitors, or weight scales with cellular connectivity
Documentation Requirements: 99453 (setup, once), 99454 (device supply/data transmission, monthly), 99457 (first 20 min clinical staff time/month reviewing data), 99458 (each additional 20 min); device must transmit data ≥16 days/month
Notes / Gotchas: Requires RPM device infrastructure. High ongoing revenue for practices with BP or glucose monitoring programs. 16-day transmission minimum is key compliance requirement.

Eligible Population: Medicare patients requiring monitoring of respiratory system or musculoskeletal therapy adherence
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients with respiratory or MSK conditions using monitoring devices (e.g., smart inhalers, PT exercise tracking)
Documentation Requirements: 98975 (setup, once), 98976/98977 (device supply, monthly), 98980 (first 20 min treatment management), 98981 (each additional 20 min); ≥16 days data/month
Notes / Gotchas: Similar to RPM but for therapeutic monitoring. Can be billed by non-physician providers. Newer codes — verify payer acceptance.

Eligible Population: All Medicare beneficiaries, annually
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag all Medicare patients without G0444 in past 12 months; prompt at AWV or any qualifying visit
Documentation Requirements: PHQ-2 or PHQ-9; document screening tool used, score, and follow-up plan; must have follow-up plan if positive
Notes / Gotchas: Standalone Medicare code separate from 96127. Can be billed during AWV. Zero cost-share.

Eligible Population: Medicare beneficiaries at high risk: diabetes, family history of glaucoma, African-Americans ≥50, Hispanic-Americans ≥65
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag Medicare patients with diabetes, family history of glaucoma, or demographic risk factors; no glaucoma screening in past 12 months
Documentation Requirements: G0117 (by optometrist/ophthalmologist — referral); G0118 (by physician under direct supervision); dilated eye exam with intraocular pressure measurement
Notes / Gotchas: Generates referral to ophthalmology. Office captures the screening order and any associated E/M. Not zero cost-share.

Eligible Population: All female Medicare beneficiaries
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag female Medicare patients without pelvic exam in past 12 months (or 24 months for low-risk)
Documentation Requirements: Clinical breast exam + pelvic exam; separate from E/M; document breast and pelvic exam findings
Notes / Gotchas: Separately billable from E/M when performed during a preventive visit. Often bundled incorrectly.

Eligible Population: Medicare Part B beneficiaries with BMI ≥25 (or ≥23 if Asian), pre-diabetes lab values, no prior diabetes diagnosis or ESRD
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag Medicare patients with pre-diabetes (A1C 5.7-6.4%, FPG 110-125, 2hr GTT 140-199) and BMI ≥25; no diabetes diagnosis
Documentation Requirements: CDC-recognized DPP supplier required; multiple session codes based on attendance and weight loss milestones; G9873 (core sessions), G9874-G9891 (various milestone codes)
Notes / Gotchas: Performance-based payment — higher reimbursement if patient achieves weight loss targets. Practice must be CDC-recognized DPP supplier or partner with one.

Eligible Population: Part of IPPE (Medicare); patients with cardiac symptoms or risk factors
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients at IPPE visits; flag patients with new cardiac symptoms, pre-operative evaluation, or medication monitoring (e.g., QTc-prolonging drugs)
Documentation Requirements: 93000 = tracing + interpretation; if only interpreting external tracing, use 93010; document indication, findings, and clinical interpretation
Notes / Gotchas: Part of the IPPE benefit for new Medicare patients. Can be billed with modifier 25 + separate E/M if distinct problem is addressed.

Eligible Population: Patients with respiratory symptoms (cough, dyspnea, wheezing); COPD monitoring; asthma management
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients with COPD or asthma diagnoses without spirometry in past 12 months; flag new respiratory symptom presentations
Frequency: Annual for COPD/asthma; as indicated for new symptoms
Documentation Requirements: Document indication, pre- and post-bronchodilator values (FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC ratio), interpretation; calibration compliance
Notes / Gotchas: In-office spirometry equipment required. High-value for practices with significant COPD/asthma population.

Eligible Population: Patients requiring rapid diagnostic testing at the visit
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag visit types where POCT is commonly indicated: URI (strep), UTI, pregnancy, glucose monitoring, influenza, COVID
Documentation Requirements: CLIA Certificate of Waiver required; document test performed, result, clinical decision based on result; common POCT: rapid strep (87880), rapid flu (87804), UA (81002/81003), urine pregnancy (81025), fingerstick glucose (82962)
Notes / Gotchas: Requires CLIA waiver. Low cost per test, high volume. Ensure each POCT is documented with indication and result.

Eligible Population: Patients requiring blood draw for lab orders
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Auto-include when office lab orders are placed and drawn in-office
Documentation Requirements: 36415 (venipuncture), 36416 (capillary blood collection); document who drew, technique, site
Notes / Gotchas: Small per-event revenue but very high volume. Ensure billing captures every in-office draw. Often missed when labs are ordered but draw is not separately billed.

Eligible Population: Patients receiving injections: vaccines, B12, Depo-Provera, steroid injections, etc.
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag all injection encounters; auto-suggest admin code alongside drug/vaccine code
Documentation Requirements: 96372 for therapeutic/prophylactic (e.g., B12, Depo, steroid, toradol); 90471 for first vaccine admin, 90472 for each additional; document drug, dose, route, site
Notes / Gotchas: Administration code is separate from the drug/vaccine product code. Both must be billed. Common miss: billing the drug but not the admin code.

Eligible Population: Patients with acute bronchospasm, asthma exacerbation, COPD exacerbation
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients receiving in-office nebulizer treatments; prompt for 94640 billing
Documentation Requirements: Document indication, medication used, pre/post assessment, patient response
Notes / Gotchas: Separate from the E/M. Can be billed alongside the office visit with modifier 25 on the E/M if a separately identifiable problem is addressed.

Eligible Population: Patients with respiratory complaints, monitoring during procedures
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag when pulse ox is performed during acute respiratory visits
Documentation Requirements: 94760 (single determination), 94761 (multiple determinations/continuous); document indication and results
Notes / Gotchas: Often documented but not billed separately. Check if payer bundles with E/M.

Eligible Population: Patients with diabetes or pre-diabetes
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag diabetic patients without A1C in past 3-6 months; flag pre-diabetic patients without A1C in past 12 months
Documentation Requirements: Document diagnosis, current A1C, target A1C, management plan adjustments based on result
Notes / Gotchas: Preventive screening (no diabetes diagnosis) may be zero cost-share with modifier 33. Monitoring for known diabetes is standard cost-sharing.

Eligible Population: Patients on statin therapy or with hyperlipidemia
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients on statins without lipid panel in past 12 months; flag hyperlipidemia patients per monitoring schedule
Documentation Requirements: Total cholesterol, LDL, HDL, triglycerides; document current therapy, response to treatment, plan adjustments
Notes / Gotchas: Preventive screening is zero cost-share. Monitoring for known hyperlipidemia is standard cost-sharing. Code the diagnosis correctly.

Eligible Population: Patients on thyroid replacement therapy; patients with thyroid disorders
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients on levothyroxine without TSH in past 6-12 months; flag patients with thyroid disorder diagnosis
Documentation Requirements: Document current thyroid medication and dose, TSH result, dose adjustment if needed
Notes / Gotchas: Very common in family practice. Ensure monitoring labs are ordered and billed at appropriate intervals.

Eligible Population: Patients on metformin, ACE/ARB, diuretics, lithium, or other nephrotoxic medications; CKD patients
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients on medications requiring renal monitoring without BMP/CMP in past 6-12 months
Documentation Requirements: Document medication monitoring indication; BMP (80048) or CMP (80053); review and document interpretation
Notes / Gotchas: Medication safety monitoring justifies the lab. Document the specific medication requiring monitoring.

Eligible Population: Patients on medications requiring hematologic monitoring (methotrexate, carbamazepine, clozapine, etc.); anemia follow-up
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients on medications requiring CBC monitoring without recent CBC per drug-specific monitoring schedule
Documentation Requirements: Document medication monitoring indication; review differential, hemoglobin, platelet count; document interpretation and plan
Notes / Gotchas: Drug monitoring schedule varies: methotrexate (q1-3 months), clozapine (weekly to monthly per registry), etc.

Eligible Population: Patients on warfarin
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients on warfarin without INR in appropriate interval (weekly to monthly depending on stability)
Documentation Requirements: Document current warfarin dose, INR result, target range, dose adjustment; for POCT INR (93792, 93793) if using in-office device
Notes / Gotchas: If office has POCT INR device, captures both lab and management codes. High-frequency billing for warfarin patients.

Eligible Population: Patients on hepatotoxic medications (statins, methotrexate, anti-epileptics, etc.); liver disease monitoring
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients on hepatotoxic medications without LFTs per monitoring schedule; flag chronic liver disease patients
Documentation Requirements: AST, ALT, alkaline phosphatase, total bilirubin, albumin; document medication monitoring indication and interpretation
Notes / Gotchas: Statin monitoring guidelines have relaxed (no longer routine monitoring for all), but still indicated with symptoms or risk factors.

Eligible Population: Diabetic patients; CKD patients; hypertensive patients with kidney risk
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag diabetic patients without UACR in past 12 months; flag CKD patients per staging/monitoring schedule
Frequency: Annual for diabetes; per CKD staging
Documentation Requirements: Urine albumin (82043) + urine creatinine (82570) for ratio; document diabetic nephropathy screening or CKD monitoring indication
Notes / Gotchas: ADA recommends annual UACR screening for all diabetic patients. Commonly missed quality metric.

Eligible Population: Patients with osteoporosis, CKD, malabsorption, on vitamin D supplementation; patients at risk for deficiency
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients on vitamin D supplementation without level check; flag osteoporosis/CKD patients; flag patients with falls risk
Documentation Requirements: Document indication for testing (not a routine screen per USPSTF); 25-hydroxyvitamin D preferred test
Notes / Gotchas: Not a USPSTF-recommended universal screen. Must have clinical indication. Payers may deny without proper diagnosis code.

Eligible Population: Established patients requiring medical discussion by telephone when visit is not appropriate
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag phone encounters with clinical decision-making that exceed 5 minutes; prompt for time-based telephone E/M code
Documentation Requirements: 99441 (5-10 min), 99442 (11-20 min), 99443 (21-30 min); document time spent, clinical issues discussed, medical decision-making, plan; cannot bill if call results in decision for in-person visit within 24 hours
Notes / Gotchas: Often overlooked revenue source. Must be initiated by the patient or guardian. Cannot be billed within 7 days of a related E/M or within 24 hours before a related E/M.

Eligible Population: Established patients communicating through patient portal/secure messaging requiring clinical time
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag portal messages requiring >5 min cumulative clinical time over 7-day period; prompt for digital E/M billing
Documentation Requirements: 99421 (5-10 min over 7 days), 99422 (11-20 min), 99423 (21+ min); cumulative time responding to patient-initiated portal communication over 7 days; document time log
Notes / Gotchas: Patient must initiate the communication. Time is cumulative over 7 calendar days. Cannot bill if communication results in a visit within 24 hours.

Eligible Population: When PCP consults with a specialist by phone/electronic on behalf of a patient
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag when provider documents specialist consultation discussion; prompt for interprofessional consult code
Documentation Requirements: 99446-99449 (specialist codes, time-based); 99452 (referring provider, 16-30 min preparation + communication); document reason for consult, specialist communicated with, time spent, outcome
Notes / Gotchas: 99452 is for the referring PCP who spends ≥16 min preparing and communicating the consultation. Often missed in primary care.

Eligible Population: All patients (increasingly recommended); special focus on chronic disease patients
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients without SDOH screening on file; prompt at new patient visits and annually at preventive visits
Documentation Requirements: 96160 = patient-focused assessment (e.g., PRAPARE tool); 96161 = caregiver-focused assessment; document tool used, results, interventions/referrals made
Notes / Gotchas: Increasingly required for quality measures. Supports Z-code documentation (Z59.x food insecurity, Z59.0 homelessness, etc.) which can support complexity in E/M coding.

Eligible Population: Women of reproductive age (USPSTF B recommendation)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag female patients of reproductive age at preventive visits; prompt for IPV screening tool (HITS, HARK, STaT)
Documentation Requirements: Validated screening tool; document screening performed, result, referral to support services if positive
Notes / Gotchas: Part of ACA preventive benefit. Documentation supports well-woman visit coding. Refer positive screens to local DV resources.

Eligible Population: All Medicare AWV patients (required component)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Auto-include in all AWV encounters; verify HRA is completed prior to or during visit
Documentation Requirements: Self-reported or provider-administered HRA collecting data on health status, injury risk, modifiable risk factors, urgent health needs; document completion
Notes / Gotchas: Required component of AWV — not separately billable but MUST be documented for AWV to be compliant. Pre-visit HRA completion saves time.

Eligible Population: All children per Bright Futures/AAP schedule: newborn, 3-5 days, 1/2/4/6/9/12/15/18/24/30 months, then annually 3-21 years
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag pediatric patients overdue for well-child visit per Bright Futures schedule; age-based code selection
Documentation Requirements: Age-appropriate history, physical exam, developmental surveillance, anticipatory guidance, immunizations, screening labs/instruments per Bright Futures periodicity schedule
Notes / Gotchas: Bright Futures periodicity schedule defines the complete package. Each visit is an opportunity for multiple billable add-ons (vaccines, screenings, labs).

Eligible Population: Children at risk: 12 months and 24 months per Bright Futures; Medicaid-enrolled children per state requirements
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag children at 12 and 24 month well visits without lead screening; flag Medicaid children per state mandate
Frequency: At 12 and 24 months (or per state mandate)
Documentation Requirements: Blood lead level (83655); capillary vs venous; document risk assessment; Medicaid typically mandates screening regardless of risk
Notes / Gotchas: Medicaid often mandates universal lead screening. Private payers cover per risk assessment with modifier 33.

Eligible Population: Infants at 12 months per Bright Futures; additional screenings for at-risk children
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag infants at 12-month well visit without hemoglobin/hematocrit; flag children with anemia risk factors
Documentation Requirements: Hemoglobin (85018) or hematocrit (85014); document screening indication per Bright Futures
Notes / Gotchas: Part of the 12-month well-child visit bundle. Low cost to perform with capillary sample.

Eligible Population: Children once between 9-11 years and once between 17-21 years per Bright Futures; earlier if family history of CVD
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag children 9-11 without lipid screening; flag adolescents 17-21 without lipid screening; flag children with family history of early CVD
Documentation Requirements: Non-fasting lipid panel acceptable for screening; document family history of early CVD if present
Notes / Gotchas: Two screening windows per Bright Futures. Often missed at the 17-21 year window as patients age out of pediatric visits.

Eligible Population: All children from primary tooth eruption through age 5 per USPSTF; some states cover through age 6
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag children with teeth present through age 5 at well visits without fluoride varnish
Documentation Requirements: 99188 (application of fluoride varnish by physician/non-dental provider); document teeth present, varnish applied, parental education on oral hygiene
Notes / Gotchas: Primary care providers CAN bill this — it's not just for dentists. USPSTF A recommendation. Multiple applications per year. Very easy to perform.

Eligible Population: Children ≥6 months whose water supply is fluoride-deficient (USPSTF B recommendation)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag children ≥6 months with water source assessment indicating fluoride deficiency
Documentation Requirements: Assess water fluoride level; prescribe oral fluoride supplementation if deficient; document water source assessment
Notes / Gotchas: Revenue from the prescribing visit and ongoing medication management. Document water fluoride assessment.

Eligible Population: All children 3-5 years at least once per USPSTF; Bright Futures recommends at multiple ages
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag children 3-5 without vision screening; flag per Bright Futures periodicity schedule
Documentation Requirements: 99173 (visual acuity screening), 99174/99177 (instrument-based screening, e.g., photoscreening); document screening method and results
Notes / Gotchas: Photoscreening devices (e.g., Spot Vision) can be used in younger children. Separately billable from the well-child visit.

Eligible Population: Newborns; children at ages per Bright Futures: 4, 5, 6, 8, 10 years; once between 11-14, 15-17, 18-21
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag children at Bright Futures hearing screening ages without documented screening
Documentation Requirements: 92551 (pure tone screening), 92552 (audiometry), 92567 (tympanometry); document screening method and results
Notes / Gotchas: If office has audiometer/tympanometer, direct billing. Otherwise, referral to audiology.

Eligible Population: Any visit where a separately identifiable E/M service is provided on the same day as a procedure or preventive service
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag visits where both an E/M problem is addressed AND a procedure/preventive service is performed; prompt for modifier 25 documentation
Documentation Requirements: Document the separate and distinct reason for the E/M beyond the procedure; separate HPI, exam, and MDM for the distinct problem
Notes / Gotchas: Most common modifier in primary care. Allows billing E/M + AWV, E/M + procedure, etc. on the same day. Must document a separately identifiable E/M service.

Eligible Population: Patients seen during extended hours, weekends, or holidays
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag encounters occurring outside regular office hours; prompt for appropriate after-hours code
Documentation Requirements: 99050 (after-hours, same provider, no emergency), 99051 (evening/weekend), 99053 (between 10pm-8am); document time of service
Notes / Gotchas: Revenue that many practices leave on the table. If the practice offers Saturday or evening hours, these are billable add-ons to the E/M.

Eligible Population: Medicare patients in home health, hospice, or under home care plan; requires 15+ min/month of physician/NP oversight
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients receiving home health services or in hospice with ongoing care coordination by the practice
Documentation Requirements: 99339 (15-29 min/month), 99340 (30+ min/month); document time spent reviewing reports, communicating with home health, updating care plans
Notes / Gotchas: Cannot bill same month as CCM for same patient. Different from TCM. For patients with active home health orders.

Eligible Population: Patients with chronic pain requiring ongoing management
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients with chronic pain diagnoses and opioid prescriptions; prompt for PDMP check documentation, pain agreement review, and appropriate E/M level
Documentation Requirements: Document pain assessment (location, severity, function), PDMP check, treatment plan, risk assessment, informed consent for controlled substances
Notes / Gotchas: Not a separate billing code per se, but proper documentation supports higher-level E/M coding and CCM eligibility. Also supports quality measure compliance.

Eligible Population: HIV-negative patients at high risk for HIV through sex or injection drug use (USPSTF A recommendation)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients with documented HIV risk factors; flag patients on PrEP medications for monitoring compliance
Documentation Requirements: Initial risk assessment, HIV test, renal function, pregnancy test, STI screening; quarterly follow-up: HIV test, adherence, renal function, pregnancy test, STI screening
Notes / Gotchas: The screening and monitoring labs are zero cost-share under ACA preventive benefit. The E/M visits generate ongoing revenue. Growing patient population.

Eligible Population: Pregnant women at high risk for preeclampsia (USPSTF B recommendation)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag pregnant patients with preeclampsia risk factors (history of preeclampsia, multifetal gestation, chronic HTN, diabetes, renal disease, autoimmune disease, nulliparity, age ≥35, BMI ≥30)
Documentation Requirements: Low-dose aspirin (81mg/day) after 12 weeks gestation; document risk factor assessment and aspirin recommendation
Notes / Gotchas: No direct billing code, but documentation supports OB management coding and risk assessment. ACA preventive benefit covers the aspirin.

Eligible Population: All women of reproductive age who are planning or capable of pregnancy (USPSTF A recommendation)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag female patients of reproductive age; prompt for folic acid counseling documentation
Documentation Requirements: Counsel on 0.4-0.8mg daily folic acid supplementation; document counseling and recommendation
Notes / Gotchas: Supports preventive visit documentation and quality measure compliance. ACA covers the supplement.

Eligible Population: Adults 40-75 with ≥1 CVD risk factor and ≥10% 10-year ASCVD risk (USPSTF B recommendation)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag patients 40-75 with 10-year ASCVD risk ≥10% (calculate from lipid panel + risk factors); not currently on statin
Documentation Requirements: Calculate 10-year ASCVD risk; document risk discussion and shared decision-making; prescribe moderate-dose statin if appropriate
Notes / Gotchas: The statin medication is zero cost-share under ACA preventive benefit when prescribed per USPSTF indication. Document the risk calculation.

Eligible Population: Asymptomatic pregnant women after 24 weeks gestation (USPSTF B recommendation)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag pregnant patients at 24-28 weeks without glucose challenge test/GTT on file
Documentation Requirements: Glucose challenge test or glucose tolerance test; document gestational age and screening indication
Notes / Gotchas: Standard of prenatal care but important to code correctly with modifier 33 for zero cost-sharing under ACA preventive benefit.

Eligible Population: Pregnant and postpartum persons at increased risk of perinatal depression (USPSTF B recommendation)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag pregnant patients and patients within 12 months postpartum for perinatal depression risk assessment and counseling referral
Documentation Requirements: Document risk assessment, counseling intervention or referral; 96161 (caregiver/patient-focused health risk assessment)
Notes / Gotchas: Newer USPSTF recommendation. Covers counseling interventions to prevent perinatal depression in those at increased risk.

Eligible Population: Pregnant women at 12-16 weeks gestation or first prenatal visit (USPSTF A recommendation)
Trigger / Detection Logic for NP Compani: Flag pregnant patients at first prenatal visit or 12-16 weeks without urine culture on file
Documentation Requirements: Urine culture (not just dipstick UA); document gestational age and screening indication
Notes / Gotchas: USPSTF A recommendation. Must be a urine CULTURE, not just UA. Often missed or incorrect test ordered.
